search_queries_used: site:england.nhs.uk adult breathlessness pathway diagnostic support tool
site:nice.org.uk chronic heart failure dyspnoea assessment recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
A broad dyspnoea presentation does not establish pulmonary embolism.
Treatment and dose content was not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: the primary pathway is for adults with chronic persistent breathlessness, while the legacy workflow has no explicit adult or chronic-only restriction.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for primary-care pre-diagnosis assessment and selected heart-failure review fields, not acute emergency treatment.
UAE_applicability: International NHS and NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local escalation, referral, and prescribing governance.
recency_verification: The official NHS England pathway and current NICE NG106 recommendations were rechecked on 2026-07-13; the regional NHS pathway remains supporting evidence only.
superseded_check: NICE NG106 remains current; no newer official NHS England adult-breathlessness support tool or regional pathway copy was identified in the verified search.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult nonacute dyspnoea and breathlessness presentation terms.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines chronic adult breathlessness as a primary-care presentation and limits the pathway to chronic rather than acute or subacute symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped symptom, assessed-negative, vital-sign, cardiopulmonary examination, and initial-test fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies onset and associated history, functional impact, exposures, medication context, vital signs, respiratory effort, cardiac and chest examination, and selected investigations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-performed or reviewed ECG, oxygen, vital-sign, blood-count, renal, and chest-imaging fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies history, physical examination, and clinician-selected initial investigations for chronic persistent breathlessness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented safety and escalation fields without asserting a diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists chest pain, haemoptysis, cyanosis, inability to speak in sentences, confusion, unilateral leg swelling, stridor, rapid progression, and low oxygen saturation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped natriuretic-peptide and echocardiography review fields in an adult heart-failure context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinical history and examination, NT-proBNP, echocardiography, ECG, chest X-ray, renal, thyroid, liver, lipid, glucose, blood count, and urinalysis review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Was reviewed for chronic cardiac monitoring context; no additional generic dyspnoea item is mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of functional capacity, fluid status, rhythm, cognition, nutrition, medication, renal function, haemoglobin or iron status, deterioration, monitoring frequency, and support needs.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped orthopnoea or PND, oedema, JVP, and peripheral-oedema documentation in worsening heart failure.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports worsening breathlessness, orthopnoea or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea, weight increase, oedema or abdominal distension, blood pressure, heart rate, oxygen saturation, JVP, pulmonary findings, and peripheral oedema documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-decided safety-netting and referral documentation for high-risk deterioration.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documentation of high-risk deterioration and clinician-led urgent referral or escalation without mapping dose-bearing treatment content.

unresolved_source_gaps: Acute dyspnoea, children, and emergency treatment are outside the primary pathway scope.
Syncope, D-dimer, tracheal position, generic clinician plans, medication or diuretic plans, and generic or fixed follow-up remain unsupported.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
